steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank fills in a noun phrase where Mark A. Lee identifies which researcher led the study. This is a restrictive appositive — it narrows down exactly who, so no commas around it. Periods after initials must also be present.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — no period after "A": Missing period after the middle initial. ✗
  • Choice B — comma after the name: Inserts a comma that separates the appositive from the rest of the sentence incorrectly. ✗
  • Choice C — "researcher Mark A. Lee": Restrictive appositive with no commas and correct punctuation on the initial. ✓
  • Choice D — no period after "A": Same initial-period error as A. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
